name: Black Bean Tacos
description: Lightly refried black beans with aromatic veggies and spices in warm corn tortillas. Based on the highly rated Love & Lemons recipe.

ingredients.name: plain Greek yogurt or sour cream

steps: Reserve ½ cup of liquid from one can of black beans, then drain and rinse both cans.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, jalapeño, garlic, and salt. Cook stirring occasionally for 8 minutes until softened.
Stir in taco seasoning and cook 1 minute. Add beans and ¼ cup of the reserved bean liquid. Cook 5–8 minutes until thick, mashing some beans with the back of a spoon.
Warm tortillas directly over a gas burner 30 seconds per side, or in a dry skillet.
Mix Greek yogurt with lime juice to make a quick crema. Assemble tacos with bean filling, cabbage, avocado, crema, and cilantro.

proteins.name: Skirt steak strips
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, and cumin. Cook in a hot cast iron skillet 2–3 min per side. Rest 5 min then slice thin against the grain.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless)
proteins.instructions: Pound thin. Season with taco seasoning. Cook in oiled skillet over medium-high 4–5 min per side.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Toss with chili powder, cumin, salt. Cook in hot skillet 1–2 min per side until pink.

name: Veggie Burrito Bowls
description: Fajita-spiced peppers and onions over rice with pinto beans and all your favorite toppings. A complete weeknight bowl.

ingredients.name: pinto beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed
bell peppers (red, yellow, or orange), sliced
fresh salsa or pico de gallo

steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add peppers and onion with fajita seasoning. Cook 7–8 minutes tossing occasionally until softened and lightly charred.
Warm beans in a small saucepan over medium heat with a splash of water and a pinch of salt, 4–5 minutes.
Cook rice packets in microwave per package directions (usually 90 seconds).
Build bowls: rice base, then peppers and onions, beans, cheese, salsa, avocado, and sour cream. Finish with lime juice.

proteins.name: Chicken breast (sliced)
proteins.instructions: Slice thin. Toss with fajita seasoning. Cook in same skillet as peppers 6–7 min. Remove before cooking veg, or cook alongside.

proteins.name: Ground beef 80/20
proteins.instructions: Brown in a separate skillet over medium-high, breaking up, 8–10 min. Drain fat. Season with 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chili powder.

proteins.name: Chorizo (Mexican-style)
proteins.instructions: Remove casing. Brown in skillet over medium 7–8 min, breaking up. Drain excess fat.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a rich, spiced tomato and bell pepper sauce. Based on the widely praised Kitchn recipe with 14oz crushed tomatoes and harissa.

ingredients.name: medium red bell pepper, diced (about 1 cup)
fresh cilantro or flat-leaf parsley, chopped
pita or crusty bread, for serving

steps: Heat olive oil in a large deep skillet over medium heat. Add onion and bell pepper and cook stirring occasionally 6–8 minutes until translucent and softened.
Add garlic, tomato paste, and harissa. Cook stirring until tomato paste darkens slightly, about 2 minutes.
Add crushed tomatoes. Pour ¼ cup water into the empty can, swirl, and add to the skillet. Add lemon juice, cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine.
Simmer over medium-low, stirring often, for 15 minutes until sauce thickens.
Use a spoon to make 6 wells in the sauce. Crack one egg into each well. Cover and cook 4–8 minutes depending on how set you like the yolks.
Remove from heat. Top with feta, fresh herbs, and extra red pepper flakes if desired. Serve immediately with pita.

proteins.name: Merguez sausage
proteins.instructions: Slice into rounds. Brown in a separate pan 5–6 min. Add directly to your bowl on top of the shakshuka.

proteins.name: Ground lamb
proteins.instructions: Brown in separate pan with a pinch of cumin and salt 7–8 min. Spoon onto your portion before adding eggs to the sauce, or serve alongside.

proteins.name: Halloumi (pan-fried)
proteins.instructions: Slice ¼-inch thick. Cook in a dry nonstick skillet 1–2 min per side until golden. Lay over the top of your portion.

name: Greek Grain Bowl
description: Nutty farro with cucumber, cherry tomatoes, kalamata olives, and feta in a bright lemon-herb dressing.
steps: Cook farro packets per package directions (usually 10 minutes in boiling water). Drain and spread out briefly to cool.
While farro cooks, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, oregano, garlic, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Add farro to the dressing bowl and toss. Add tomatoes, cucumber, olives, roasted peppers, and parsley. Toss again.
Divide into bowls and top generously with crumbled feta. Serve warm or at room temperature.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, and oregano. Cook in oiled skillet over medium-high 5–6 min per side. Rest 5 min, slice, and lay over your bowl.

proteins.name: Lamb loin chops
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder. Cook in a hot cast iron 3–4 min per side for medium. Rest before slicing.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Toss with olive oil, oregano, lemon zest, salt, pepper. Sear in hot skillet 1–2 min per side.

name: Pasta e Fagioli
description: Classic Italian soup with two types of beans, small pasta, crushed tomatoes, and Parmesan. Based on the Cookie & Kate and Love & Lemons recipes with 250+ five-star reviews.

ingredients.name: ditalini or small shell pasta, dry
cannellini beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed
Parmesan rind (from a block) + more grated to serve

steps: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, celery, and ½ tsp salt. Cook stirring often 6–8 minutes until soft.
Add garlic, rosemary, oregano, and red pepper flakes. Stir 1 minute until fragrant.
Add crushed tomatoes and stir. Add beans, broth, Parmesan rind, remaining salt, and several grinds of black pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Cook 10 minutes. Scoop out 1½ cups of soup (avoiding the Parmesan rind), blend until smooth, and stir back in for a creamy texture.
Add dry pasta and simmer 10 more minutes until al dente. Remove Parmesan rind. Stir in parsley. Taste and adjust salt.
Serve with grated Parmesan and a drizzle of olive oil. Note: pasta absorbs broth as leftovers sit — add a splash of broth when reheating.

proteins.name: Italian sausage links
proteins.instructions: Slice into rounds. Brown in a separate skillet over medium 7–8 min. Serve alongside or spoon onto your bowl.

proteins.name: Pancetta, diced
proteins.instructions: Cook in a small skillet over medium until crispy, 6–7 min. Drain on paper towels. Crumble over your bowl at the end.

proteins.name: Ground beef 80/20
proteins.instructions: Brown in a separate pan with a pinch of oregano and salt, 8–10 min. Drain fat. Spoon onto your bowl.

name: Zucchini Aglio e Olio
description: The classic Roman pasta — spaghetti in garlic-infused olive oil with crispy golden zucchini, lemon zest, and Parmesan. Simple, elegant, and fast.

ingredients.name: medium zucchini, sliced into thin rounds

steps: Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Salt generously (1 tbsp). Cook spaghetti 1 minute less than package directions. Reserve 1½ cups pasta water before draining.

While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium. Add sliced garlic and cook slowly 3–4 minutes until golden (not brown). Add red pepper flakes and cook 30 seconds. Remove garlic with a slotted spoon and set aside.
Raise heat to medium-high. Add zucchini slices in a single layer with a pinch of salt. Cook undisturbed 2 minutes per side until golden. Work in batches if needed.
Add drained pasta to the skillet. Add ½ cup pasta water and toss vigorously to emulsify the oil and water into a glossy sauce. Add more pasta water as needed.
Remove from heat. Add lemon zest, lemon juice, Parmesan, and parsley. Toss to coat. Top with the reserved golden garlic. Season with black pepper and serve immediately.

proteins.name: Large shrimp (peeled, deveined)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, lemon zest. Cook in a separate pan in butter and garlic 1–2 min per side. Pile on top of your portion.

proteins.name: Chicken breast (thin-cut or pounded)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper. Cook in oiled skillet over medium-high 3–4 min per side until golden. Slice and lay over your bowl.

proteins.name: Italian sausage (removed from casing)
proteins.instructions: Brown and crumble in a separate skillet over medium, 7–8 min. Drain and spoon onto your portion.

name: Thai Peanut Noodles
description: Rice noodles tossed in a creamy, sweet-spicy peanut sauce with shredded carrots, cucumber, and edamame. Based on the widely loved Nora Cooks recipe.

ingredients.name: English cucumber, julienned or thinly sliced

steps: Cook rice noodles according to package directions. Drain and rinse well under cold water to prevent sticking. Toss with ½ tsp sesame oil while still warm.
Whisk together peanut butter, soy sauce, lime juice, rice vinegar, honey, sriracha, garlic, and sesame oil in a bowl until smooth. If the sauce is too thick, add 1–2 tbsp warm water.
Add noodles to a large bowl with edamame, cucumber, carrots, and green onions. Pour peanut sauce over and toss to coat evenly.
Divide into bowls. Top with chopped peanuts, sesame seeds, and cilantro. Serve with extra lime wedges and sriracha on the side.

proteins.name: Chicken breast, sliced thin
proteins.instructions: Poach in barely simmering salted water 12 min, or pan-sear in oil 3–4 min per side over medium-high. Shred or slice and fan over your bowl.

proteins.name: Large shrimp (peeled)
proteins.instructions: Toss with a little sesame oil, salt, pinch of red pepper. Sear in hot skillet 1–2 min per side. Lay on top of your bowl.

proteins.name: Ground pork
proteins.instructions: Brown in skillet over medium-high 7–8 min, breaking up. Season with 1 tbsp soy sauce, pinch of ginger. Spoon onto your bowl.

name: Miso Soup & Rice Bowl
description: Umami-rich miso broth with silken tofu and wakame over steamed rice. One of the fastest warming meals in the rotation.

ingredients.name: low-sodium vegetable broth or dashi broth

steps: Soak dried wakame in cold water for 5 minutes. Drain and squeeze out excess water. Set aside.
Heat broth in a medium saucepan over medium heat until steaming but not boiling. Remove from heat.

In a small bowl, whisk miso paste with a ladleful of hot broth until completely dissolved. Stir back into the pot. Do not boil after adding miso — it kills the probiotic cultures and changes the flavor.
Gently add tofu cubes and wakame. Warm 2 minutes over low heat.
Heat rice packets per package directions. Divide rice into bowls, ladle miso soup alongside or over the rice. Top with green onions, nori strips, sesame seeds, and a drizzle of sesame oil.

proteins.name: Salmon fillet
proteins.instructions: Pat dry. Season skin-side with salt. Cook skin-side down in oiled skillet over medium-high 4–5 min until crispy. Flip and cook 2–3 min more. Lay over rice.

proteins.name: Teriyaki chicken thighs
proteins.instructions: Brush with 2 tbsp teriyaki sauce. Cook in oiled skillet over medium 5–6 min per side until cooked through. Slice and fan over your bowl.

proteins.name: Pork tenderloin, thin-sliced
proteins.instructions: Slice ½-inch thick. Season with soy sauce, ginger, and a pinch of sugar. Sear in hot skillet 2 min per side.

name: Three-Bean Veggie Chili
description: A hearty three-bean chili with chipotle, fire-roasted tomatoes, and smoky spices. Genuinely satisfying without meat.

ingredients.name: black beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed
kidney beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed
pinto beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed
chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, minced
fresh cilantro and lime, for topping

steps: Heat olive oil in a large heavy pot over medium heat. Add onion and a pinch of salt. Cook 4–5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic, chipotle peppers, chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and oregano. Stir 1–2 minutes until fragrant and darkened.
Add all three cans of beans, crushed tomatoes, and broth. Stir to combine. Bring to a simmer.
Cook uncovered over medium-low for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Use the back of a spoon to mash a portion of the beans against the pot wall for thicker texture.
Taste and adjust salt, chipotle, and spices. Serve topped with cheddar, sour cream, and cilantro.

proteins.name: Ground beef 80/20
proteins.instructions: Brown in a separate skillet over medium-high, breaking up, 8–10 min. Drain fat. Season with 1 tsp chili powder + salt. Spoon into your bowl on top.

proteins.name: Ground turkey
proteins.instructions: Cook in separate skillet over medium 8–10 min, breaking up and seasoning with chili powder, cumin, and salt. Drain any liquid.

proteins.name: Bison (ground)
proteins.instructions: Brown in separate skillet 7–8 min with a pinch of chili powder and salt. Leaner than beef — don't overcook.

name: Loaded Baked Potato Bar
description: Microwave baked potatoes with a full toppings bar. One of the fastest meals in the rotation with near-zero active cook time.
steps: Scrub potatoes and pierce all over with a fork (at least 8 times each). Place on a microwave-safe plate.

Microwave on high: 1 potato = 5 minutes; 2 potatoes = 8–10 minutes; 4 potatoes = 14–16 minutes. Flip halfway. Test with a knife — it should slide in easily. (Alternatively, bake at 450°F for 45–50 min for crispy skin.)
While potatoes cook, microwave broccoli steam-bag per package directions (usually 3–4 minutes).
Carefully cut each potato open lengthwise. Push ends toward center to open. Add butter, season with salt and pepper.
Set out all toppings: cheese, sour cream, broccoli, green onions, and hot sauce. Everyone builds their own.

proteins.name: Rotisserie chicken (pre-cooked)
proteins.instructions: Zero cook time. Shred and warm in a bowl in the microwave 90 seconds with a splash of chicken broth. Pile on top.

proteins.name: Bacon strips
proteins.instructions: Cook in a cold skillet over medium heat 7–8 min, flipping once. Drain on paper towels. Crumble over the potato.

proteins.name: Chili topping (leftover veggie chili)
proteins.instructions: Reheat leftover chili in a saucepan 4–5 min or microwave 2 min. Ladle over the split potato.

name: Mediterranean Flatbread Pizzas
description: Crispy naan topped with hummus, roasted vegetables, and feta. Ready in the time it takes to heat an oven.
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F. Place naan on a baking sheet.
Spread hummus evenly over each naan, leaving a small border. Layer with spinach, cherry tomatoes, roasted peppers, and olives.
Top with crumbled feta. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with oregano and red pepper flakes.
Bake 10–12 minutes until the edges are golden and crispy and the feta is slightly golden.
Remove from oven. Finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice and torn fresh basil or parsley.

proteins.name: Rotisserie chicken (shredded)
proteins.instructions: Scatter shredded chicken over the hummus before adding vegetables. It heats through during the 10-12 min bake.

proteins.name: Gyro-style beef or lamb strips
proteins.instructions: Slice thin. Season with cumin, oregano, garlic powder, salt. Sear in hot skillet 3–4 min. Arrange on your naan after baking.

proteins.name: Merguez sausage, sliced
proteins.instructions: Brown slices in skillet 4–5 min. Scatter over your flatbread after baking.

name: Lemon Butter Orzo with Spinach
description: A bright one-skillet orzo finished with butter, parmesan, lemon, and wilted spinach. Inspired by the highly rated Half Baked Harvest lemon butter orzo.
steps: Melt 2 tbsp butter with olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes; cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

Add orzo and toast 1–2 minutes, stirring. Pour in broth and lemon zest. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 12–15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until orzo is tender and most of the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in spinach a handful at a time; let it wilt 1–2 minutes.
Off heat, stir in the remaining 2 tbsp butter, lemon juice, and parmesan until creamy. Season with salt and pepper.
Top with feta and fresh herbs. Serve immediately.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, and lemon zest. Sear in oiled skillet 5–6 min per side until 165°F. Slice and serve over orzo.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Toss with olive oil, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt. Sauté in hot skillet 1–2 min per side until pink and curled.

proteins.name: Tofu (extra-firm, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Press, then pat dry. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil and 1 tsp salt. Pan-fry 8–10 min, turning, until golden on all sides.

name: White Bean & Tomato Skillet with Feta
description: Creamy white beans simmered with blistered cherry tomatoes, olives, spinach, and feta — served with crusty bread. Based on the popular Mediterranean Dish skillet technique.

ingredients.name: cannellini or white beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed

steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium. Add onion with a pinch of salt; cook 5 minutes until soft.
Add garlic, oregano, and red pepper flakes; cook 30 seconds.
Stir in cherry tomatoes; cook 4–5 minutes, smashing some with the back of a spoon to release their juices.
Add beans and olives; simmer 5 minutes to thicken slightly.
Stir in spinach until just wilted, 1–2 minutes. Off heat, finish with lemon juice and parsley.
Top with feta. Serve with warm bread to mop up the sauce.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, oregano. Sear in oiled skillet 5–6 min per side until 165°F. Slice and serve alongside.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Toss with olive oil, smoked paprika, and salt. Sauté in hot skillet 1–2 min per side until pink.

proteins.name: Italian-style chicken sausage, sliced
proteins.instructions: Brown slices in oiled skillet over medium 5–6 min until caramelized on both sides.

name: Spanakorizo (Greek Spinach Rice)
description: A classic Greek one-pot of rice, tomatoes, and a huge amount of spinach finished with dill, lemon, and feta. Based on the widely loved Kitchn version of this traditional dish.
steps: Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a Dutch oven or deep skillet over medium. Add onion and a pinch of salt; cook 6 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add garlic; cook 30 seconds. Stir in rice and toast 1 minute.
Pour in diced tomatoes (with juices) and broth. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 15 minutes.
Stir in spinach by handfuls — it looks like a lot but wilts way down. Cover and cook 3–4 more minutes until spinach is tender and rice is fluffy.
Off heat, stir in lemon juice, fresh herbs, and the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil. Top with feta and serve.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, oregano, and lemon zest. Sear in oiled skillet 5–6 min per side until 165°F. Slice.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Toss with olive oil, garlic, and salt. Sauté in hot skillet 1–2 min per side until pink.

proteins.name: Chickpeas (15oz can), drained
proteins.instructions: For a vegetarian boost: warm chickpeas with a drizzle of olive oil, lemon zest, and a pinch of cumin in a skillet 3–4 min.

name: Pasta e Ceci
description: A Roman classic — pasta cooked right in a brothy chickpea-tomato base with rosemary and parmesan. Smashed chickpeas thicken it into something between a stew and a sauce. Based on Smitten Kitchen's quick version.

ingredients.name: olive oil, plus more to finish
grated parmesan, plus more for serving

steps: Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium. Add onion with a pinch of salt; cook 5 minutes until soft.
Stir in garlic, rosemary, red pepper flakes, and tomato paste. Cook 2 minutes until the paste darkens slightly.
Add chickpeas and broth. Bring to a boil, then simmer 5 minutes. Use a fork or potato masher to crush about half the chickpeas to thicken the broth.

Add pasta. Cook 10–12 minutes (per package time), stirring often, until pasta is al dente and the mixture has thickened to a porridge-like consistency. Loosen with a splash of broth or water if needed.
Off heat, remove the rosemary sprig. Stir in parmesan, salt, and pepper. Drizzle with olive oil and serve with extra parm.

proteins.name: Italian pork sausage, casings removed
proteins.instructions: Brown in a separate skillet over medium-high 6–8 min, breaking up. Drain fat. Stir into the stew at the end.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, and fennel seed. Sauté in oiled skillet 7–8 min until cooked through. Stir in at the end.

proteins.name: Pancetta or bacon, diced
proteins.instructions: Render in a cold skillet, slowly bringing to medium heat, 8–10 min until crisp. Drain on paper towel and scatter over bowls.

name: Tuscan White Bean Pasta with Kale
description: One-pot pasta with cannellini beans, sun-dried tomatoes, garlic, and lacinato kale, finished with lemon and parmesan. Inspired by the highly rated Bon Appétit Tuscan-style pasta technique.

ingredients.name: cannellini beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed
lacinato kale, stems removed, leaves chopped

steps: Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium. Add onion with a pinch of salt; cook 5 minutes.
Stir in garlic, red pepper flakes, and sun-dried tomatoes; cook 1 minute.
Add pasta, beans, and broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a strong simmer. Cook uncovered 10–12 minutes, stirring frequently, until pasta is al dente and the broth has thickened.
Stir in kale; cook 3–4 minutes until wilted and tender. Add a splash of broth or water if the pan gets dry.
Off heat, stir in parmesan, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Season to taste. Top with basil and extra parm.

proteins.name: Italian sausage links, sliced
proteins.instructions: Brown slices in a separate skillet over medium 6–8 min until caramelized. Stir into pasta at the end.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder. Sear 5 min per side. Rest, then slice and serve over the pasta.

proteins.name: White beans (extra 15oz can)
proteins.instructions: For a heartier vegetarian version, add a second drained can of cannellini with the kale.

name: Pasta all'Arrabbiata
description: Spicy Roman tomato pasta with lots of garlic, chili, and olive oil — five ingredients, no compromise. Based on the classic Bon Appétit arrabbiata.

ingredients.name: whole peeled tomatoes (28oz can), crushed by hand
Calabrian chili paste (or 1 tsp crushed red pepper flakes)

steps: Bring a large pot of well-salted water to a boil. Cook pasta 1 minute less than package directions for al dente.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low. Add garlic and chili paste; cook 3–4 minutes, stirring, until garlic is fragrant and just golden — don't let it burn.
Add crushed tomatoes with their juices and 1 tsp salt. Simmer 8–10 minutes until thickened, smashing any large pieces.

Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain pasta. Add pasta to the skillet with ½ cup pasta water. Toss vigorously 1–2 minutes over medium heat, adding more pasta water as needed, until the sauce coats every strand.
Off heat, stir in half the parsley and half the cheese. Plate and top with the rest of the parsley, cheese, and a drizzle of olive oil.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Sauté shrimp in 1 tbsp olive oil with a clove of garlic 1–2 min per side until pink. Toss with the finished pasta.

proteins.name: Italian sausage, casings removed
proteins.instructions: Brown sausage in the sauce skillet 6–8 min, breaking up. Add the garlic and chili, then proceed with the sauce.

proteins.name: Chickpeas (15oz can), drained
proteins.instructions: For a vegetarian version: toast chickpeas in 1 tbsp olive oil with a pinch of salt 4–5 min until lightly crisp. Toss with pasta.

name: Korean Rice Bowls
description: Bulgogi-inspired rice bowls with a sweet-savory soy and gochujang glaze, kimchi, quick-sautéed veg, and sesame. Based on the highly rated Kitchn Korean beef bowl.
steps: Cook rice per package directions (about 15–18 minutes).
While rice cooks, whisk soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, gochujang, vinegar, garlic, and ginger in a small bowl. Set the sauce aside.
Cook your protein per the add-on instructions. Pour the sauce over once it's browned and let it bubble 1–2 minutes to glaze.
Briefly sauté carrots in neutral oil 2 minutes until crisp-tender; wilt spinach in the residual heat off the flame.
Build bowls: rice, glazed protein, sautéed carrots and spinach, kimchi, scallions, sesame seeds.

proteins.name: Ground beef 85/15
proteins.instructions: Brown in a skillet over medium-high 6–8 min, breaking up. Drain excess fat. Pour sauce over and simmer 1–2 min to glaze.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Sauté in 1 tbsp oil 7–8 min until cooked through. Add sauce; simmer 1 min until glossy.

proteins.name: Tofu (extra-firm, cubed and pressed)
proteins.instructions: Pat dry. Pan-fry in 1 tbsp oil 8–10 min, turning, until golden on all sides. Toss with sauce off heat.

name: Veggie Teriyaki Stir-Fry
description: Crisp-tender broccoli, peppers, snap peas, and carrots tossed in a glossy homemade teriyaki glaze. Based on Cookie + Kate's vegetable teriyaki technique with a fresh ginger-garlic base.
steps: Cook rice per package directions.
Whisk soy sauce, maple syrup, vinegar, sesame oil, cornstarch, and water in a small bowl. Set the sauce aside.
Heat neutral oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high. Add broccoli; stir-fry 2 minutes. Add bell pepper, carrots, and snap peas; stir-fry 3–4 minutes until crisp-tender.
Add garlic and ginger; cook 30 seconds. Pour in the sauce and toss to coat. Simmer 1 minute until glossy and thickened.
Serve over rice. Finish with sesame seeds and scallions.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Toss with 1 tbsp cornstarch and a pinch of salt. Pan-fry in oiled skillet 6–7 min until golden. Add to stir-fry with the sauce.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Quick-sauté in 1 tbsp oil 1–2 min per side until pink. Add to the stir-fry at the very end.

proteins.name: Tofu (extra-firm, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Toss with 1 tbsp cornstarch and a pinch of salt. Pan-fry 8–10 min until crispy on all sides. Toss with the sauce.

name: Sesame Tahini Noodle Stir-Fry
description: Ramen noodles and a mountain of spring vegetables tossed in a punchy tahini-orange-soy sauce with edamame and sesame. Inspired by The Kitchn's springy noodle stir-fry — bold, glossy, and loaded.

ingredients.name: ramen or lo mein noodles (10–12 oz)
asparagus, trimmed and sliced on diagonal into 1-inch pieces

steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook noodles per package directions. Reserve ¼ cup pasta water, then drain.

Whisk together tahini, orange juice, soy sauce, ginger, garlic, and sriracha in a large bowl until smooth. Season with salt. Add edamame and half the vegetables (snap peas, carrots); toss to coat in the sauce.

Heat neutral oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high until shimmering. Add remaining vegetables (asparagus, bell pepper) with a pinch of salt. Stir-fry 3–4 minutes until lightly charred and crisp-tender.
Add drained noodles and the sauce bowl contents to the wok. Toss everything together over medium-high heat 1–2 minutes, adding a splash of reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce into a glossy coat.
Pile into bowls. Top with sesame seeds, green onions, and extra sriracha to taste.

proteins.name: Large shrimp (peeled, deveined)
proteins.instructions: Toss with a pinch of salt and sesame oil. Sear in a hot oiled skillet 1–2 min per side until pink. Add over noodles at the end.

proteins.name: Chicken breast, thinly sliced
proteins.instructions: Season with salt and a splash of soy sauce. Stir-fry in oiled wok over medium-high 5–6 min until cooked through. Toss with the noodles.

proteins.name: Extra-firm tofu, cubed and pressed
proteins.instructions: Pat dry. Pan-fry in 2 tbsp oil over medium-high 8–10 min, turning until golden on all sides. Toss in with noodles at the end.

name: Thai Red Curry with Vegetables
description: Coconut-rich red curry loaded with broccoli, bell pepper, and spinach, finished with lime and Thai basil over jasmine rice. Based on Cookie + Kate's highly rated vegetable Thai red curry.
steps: Cook rice per package directions.
Heat oil in a Dutch oven or deep skillet over medium. Add onion and a pinch of salt; cook 4 minutes. Add garlic and ginger; cook 30 seconds.
Stir in curry paste and toast 1 minute until fragrant. Pour in coconut milk, soy sauce, and brown sugar. Bring to a simmer.
Add carrots, broccoli, and bell pepper. Simmer 6–8 minutes until vegetables are crisp-tender.
Stir in spinach and lime juice; cook 1 minute until wilted. Taste and adjust salt, sugar, or lime.
Serve over rice, topped with fresh herbs.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Sear in 1 tbsp oil 6–7 min until cooked through. Add to the curry with the bell pepper to finish simmering.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Drop raw shrimp into the simmering curry for the last 2–3 min until pink and curled.

proteins.name: Tofu (extra-firm, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Pat dry. Pan-fry in 1 tbsp oil 8–10 min until golden. Add to curry at the very end so it stays crisp.

name: Veggie Black Bean Enchiladas
description: Flour tortillas stuffed with sautéed broccoli, bell pepper, spinach, and black beans in a red enchilada sauce, topped with melted Monterey Jack. Cookie & Kate's most popular Mexican recipe.

ingredients.name: black beans (15oz can), drained and rinsed
red enchilada sauce (15oz can or homemade)
sour cream or Greek yogurt, for serving

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F. Lightly grease a 9x13 baking dish and spread ½ cup enchilada sauce on the bottom.

Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium. Add onion with a pinch of salt; cook 5 minutes. Add broccoli and bell pepper, cover, and cook 8–9 minutes until broccoli is bright green and just tender.
Add cumin and cinnamon; stir 30 seconds. Add spinach in handfuls and stir until wilted, 2 minutes. Transfer to a bowl and stir in black beans and ¼ cup cheese.
Warm tortillas 30 seconds in the microwave to make them pliable. Spoon ½ cup filling down the center of each, roll tightly, and place seam-side down in the baking dish.

Pour remaining enchilada sauce over the top and sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake 20 minutes until bubbly and cheese is melted. Let rest 5 minutes, then top with cilantro and serve with sour cream.

proteins.name: Rotisserie chicken, shredded
proteins.instructions: Stir shredded chicken into the filling before rolling. It heats through during the 20-minute bake.

proteins.name: Ground beef or turkey
proteins.instructions: Brown in a separate skillet over medium-high 7–8 min, breaking up. Season with cumin, chili powder, and salt. Mix into the filling.

proteins.name: Chorizo (Mexican-style)
proteins.instructions: Remove casing. Brown in skillet over medium 6–7 min, breaking up. Drain fat. Stir into filling before rolling.

name: Spiced Lentil Soup with Greens
description: A deeply savory, cumin-and-curry-spiced lentil soup with diced tomatoes and wilted kale, finished with a squeeze of lemon. Cookie & Kate's most-made soup — thousands of five-star reviews.

ingredients.name: lacinato kale or collard greens, stems removed and chopped

steps: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium. Add onion and carrots; cook 5 minutes until softened. Add garlic, cumin, curry powder, thyme, and red pepper flakes; stir 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes and cook 2 minutes, stirring, to deepen the flavor. Add lentils, broth, water, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil.
Partially cover, reduce heat, and simmer 25–30 minutes until lentils are tender but still hold their shape.
Transfer 2 cups of soup to a blender (or use an immersion blender briefly). Purée until smooth, then stir back in for a creamy, thick texture.
Stir in kale and cook 5 minutes until tender. Off heat, add lemon juice. Taste and adjust salt and lemon. Serve with crusty bread.

proteins.name: Merguez or lamb sausage
proteins.instructions: Slice into rounds. Brown in a skillet over medium 5–6 min. Serve on top of your bowl.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Season with cumin, salt, and pepper. Sauté in oiled skillet 7–8 min until cooked through. Ladle soup over.

proteins.name: Halloumi (pan-fried)
proteins.instructions: Slice ¼-inch thick. Cook in dry nonstick skillet 1–2 min per side until golden. Lay slices over your bowl.

name: Coconut Vegetable Curry
description: Cauliflower, sweet potato, and spinach simmered in a creamy coconut milk broth with turmeric, ginger, and curry spices. Love & Lemons' most-loved curry — warming, flexible, and weeknight-fast.

ingredients.name: red Thai chile or pinch of cayenne (optional)
fresh cilantro or basil, for serving

steps: Cook rice per package directions.

Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium. Add onion and a pinch of salt; cook 5 minutes until soft. Add garlic, ginger, and chile; cook 1 minute. Stir in curry powder, turmeric, and coriander; cook 30 seconds.
Add sweet potato and stir to coat in the spices. Add coconut milk, ½ cup water, and 1 tsp salt. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 8 minutes.
Add cauliflower, cover, and cook 10–12 minutes more until both the sweet potato and cauliflower are fork-tender.
Stir in peas and spinach; cook 2 minutes until spinach wilts and peas are warmed through. Off heat, stir in lime juice. Taste and adjust salt. Serve over rice with fresh herbs and naan.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, cubed)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt and a pinch of curry powder. Sear in oiled skillet over medium-high 6–7 min. Add to the curry with the cauliflower.

proteins.name: Large shrimp (peeled)
proteins.instructions: Drop into the simmering curry for the last 3 minutes, just until pink and curled.

proteins.name: Chickpeas (15oz can), drained
proteins.instructions: For a heartier vegetarian base: stir in drained chickpeas with the cauliflower. They'll absorb the sauce beautifully.

name: Pad Thai
description: Classic Thai stir-fried rice noodles in a tangy tamarind-fish sauce with bean sprouts, egg, and crushed peanuts. Based on the widely praised Serious Eats pad thai technique.

ingredients.name: green onions, sliced (white and green parts separated)
dried red chili flakes, for serving

steps: Soak rice noodles in room-temperature water for 30 minutes until pliable but not fully soft. Drain well.
Stir together tamarind, fish sauce, brown sugar, and sriracha in a small bowl until sugar dissolves. Set aside.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat until smoking. Add shallots and white parts of green onions; stir-fry 1 minute. Add garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Add drained noodles and the sauce. Toss and stir-fry 2–3 minutes until noodles are tender and sauce is absorbed. Push noodles to one side.
Add remaining 1 tbsp oil to the empty side. Crack in eggs and scramble 30 seconds, then fold into noodles before fully set.
Add bean sprouts and toss everything together 30 seconds — they should stay crisp. Plate and top with peanuts, green onion tops, lime wedges, and chili flakes.

proteins.name: Large shrimp (peeled, deveined)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt. Stir-fry in 1 tbsp oil over high heat 1–2 min per side until pink. Remove, then add back in with the bean sprouts at the end.

proteins.name: Chicken breast, thinly sliced
proteins.instructions: Toss with 1 tsp fish sauce. Stir-fry in oiled wok over high heat 4–5 min until cooked through. Remove then add back at the end.

proteins.name: Extra-firm tofu, cubed and pressed
proteins.instructions: Pat dry. Toss with 1 tsp fish sauce or soy sauce and 1 tbsp cornstarch. Pan-fry in 2 tbsp oil 8–10 min until crispy. Add with the bean sprouts.

name: Chinese Egg Fried Rice
description: Classic Chinese fried rice with scrambled egg, peas, scallions, and a wok-charred soy-sesame glaze. Best made with day-old rice — the fastest meal in the Asian rotation.

ingredients.name: cooked day-old jasmine rice (or 2 microwave packets, cooled)

steps: If using microwave rice, cook and spread on a plate to cool completely — cold, dry rice is the key to not getting mush. Break up any clumps with your hands.

Whisk eggs with a pinch of salt and white pepper. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large wok or skillet over high heat. Add eggs and scramble softly, 30–45 seconds — leave them slightly underdone. Transfer to a plate.
Add remaining 2 tbsp oil to the wok over high heat. Add garlic and ginger; cook 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add rice and press into a single layer. Let cook undisturbed 1–2 minutes to develop some char, then toss and repeat once more.
Pour in soy sauce and oyster sauce; toss to coat evenly. Add peas, green onions, and scrambled eggs. Toss everything together 1 minute until heated through.
Off heat, drizzle with sesame oil and toss. Taste and adjust soy. Serve immediately.

proteins.name: Char siu pork (store-bought or leftover), diced
proteins.instructions: No cooking needed — dice and toss in with the peas and green onions at the end just to heat through.

proteins.name: Chicken breast, small-diced
proteins.instructions: Season with salt and a splash of soy sauce. Stir-fry in 1 tbsp oil over high heat 5–6 min until cooked through. Remove, then add back in with the peas.

proteins.name: Large shrimp (peeled, halved)
proteins.instructions: Season with salt and white pepper. Stir-fry in 1 tbsp oil 1–2 min per side until pink. Remove, then add back in at the very end.

name: Vietnamese Lemongrass Noodle Bowls
description: Bún-style bowls with vermicelli rice noodles, quick-pickled daikon and carrots, crisp lettuce, and a punchy lemongrass-fish sauce dressing (nước chấm). Based on the highly rated Bon Appétit bún bowl technique.

ingredients.name: fresh red chili or sambal, to taste
lemongrass stalks (inner pale parts only), minced

steps: Quick-pickle the daikon and carrots: toss with rice vinegar, 1 tsp sugar, and a pinch of salt. Set aside at least 10 minutes while you prep everything else.

Make the nước chấm dressing: stir together fish sauce, lime juice, water, remaining sugar, garlic, and chili until sugar dissolves. Stir in minced lemongrass. Taste — it should be bright, salty, tangy, and a little sweet.
Cook vermicelli per package directions (usually 3–5 min in boiling water). Drain, rinse under cold water, and toss with a small drizzle of neutral oil to prevent sticking.
Cook your protein per the add-on instructions with lemongrass marinade (see proteins below).
Build bowls: noodles on the bottom, then lettuce, cucumber, pickled veg, protein, and fresh herbs. Spoon dressing generously over everything. Top with peanuts.

proteins.name: Chicken thighs (boneless, skinless)
proteins.instructions: Mince 1 additional lemongrass stalk. Marinate chicken 10 min in 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tbsp neutral oil, the minced lemongrass, and 1 tsp sugar. Grill or sear over medium-high 5–6 min per side. Slice and fan over bowls.

proteins.name: Pork tenderloin, thin-sliced
proteins.instructions: Marinate slices in 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tsp sugar, minced lemongrass, and 1 tsp sesame oil for 10 min. Sear in hot oiled skillet 2 min per side.

proteins.name: Large shrimp (peeled, deveined)
proteins.instructions: Toss with 1 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tsp sugar, 1 minced lemongrass stalk, and a splash of lime juice. Sear in hot skillet 1–2 min per side until pink.

name: Crispy Black Bean Tostadas
description: Crunchy baked tostadas piled with smoky refried black beans, a sharp feta-lime cabbage slaw, and ripe avocado. Inspired by Smitten Kitchen's crispy black bean tacos — done in 20 minutes flat.

ingredients.name: tostada shells (store-bought, or corn tortillas baked at 400°F for 8 min)

steps: Make the slaw: toss shredded cabbage with 2 tbsp lime juice, a pinch of salt, and half the feta. Let sit while you prep the beans — it softens and gets better by the minute.

Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium. Add garlic, cumin, and smoked paprika; cook 30 seconds. Add beans with about half their liquid. Cook 5–6 minutes, mashing roughly with a fork until thick and spreadable. Season with salt, hot sauce, and remaining lime juice.
Spread a thick layer of beans on each tostada shell. Top with slaw, avocado slices, remaining feta, and cilantro.
Drizzle with sour cream or serve on the side. Eat immediately — the shells stay crunchiest fresh.

proteins.name: Skirt steak, thin-sliced
proteins.instructions: Season with salt, cumin, and smoked paprika. Sear in hot cast iron 2–3 min per side. Rest 5 min, then slice thin against the grain. Fan over the tostadas.

proteins.name: Rotisserie chicken, shredded
proteins.instructions: Zero cook time. Warm in microwave 60 seconds with a splash of lime juice and a pinch of cumin. Pile on top of the beans before the slaw.

proteins.name: Shrimp (large, peeled)
proteins.instructions: Toss with olive oil, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt. Cook in hot skillet 1–2 min per side until pink. Arrange over beans.
